MiaM_Functional Analysis_PICRUST

Install pakages

library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(phyloseq)
library(ggpicrust2)
## Loading required package: ggpicrust2
## To cite ggpicrust2 in publications use:
## Chen Yang, Jiahao Mai, Xuan Cao, Aaron Burberry, Fabio Cominelli, Liangliang Zhang, ggpicrust2: an R package for PICRUSt2 predicted functional profile analysis and visualization, Bioinformatics, Volume 39, Issue 8, August 2023, btad470, https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btad470
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

Load datasets

meta = readRDS('phyloseq_taxonomy.rds') %>% 
  .@sam_data %>% data.frame() %>% 
  rownames_to_column('sample_names')

path = read.delim('path_abun_unstrat.tsv', 
                  row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)

# Keep only samples present in both metadata and pathway table
common_samples <- intersect(meta$sample_names, colnames(path))

meta <- meta %>%
  filter(sample_names %in% common_samples)

path <- path %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(common_samples))

meta <- meta %>%
  slice(match(colnames(path), sample_names))

# Set 10 days as reference level
meta$Time_of_Collection_categorical <- factor(
  meta$Time_of_Collection_categorical,
  levels = c("10_days", "30_days"))

dose_levels <- unique(meta$dose)

——————————————————–

Aim3:

Compare functional pathways between

10d vs 30d across different radiation dose groups

——————————————————–

Run differential analysis seperately for each radiation dose (0.0, 0.1, 0.25)

all_results <- lapply(dose_levels, function(d) { 
  
  meta_d <- meta %>% filter(dose == d)
 
  path_filt <- path %>% dplyr::select(all_of(meta_d$sample_names))
  
  meta_d <- meta_d %>% slice(match(colnames(path_filt), sample_names))
  
  res <- pathway_daa(
    abundance  = path_filt,
    metadata   = meta_d,
    group      = "Time_of_Collection_categorical",
    daa_method = "LinDA",
    reference  = "10_days")
  res$dose <- d
  res
})
## Using column 'sample_names' as sample identifier

## Warning in MicrobiomeStat::linda(feature.dat = feature.dat, meta.dat = meta.dat, : Some features have less than 3 nonzero values! 
##                      They have virtually no statistical power. You may consider filtering them in the analysis!
## Pseudo-count approach is used.
## Fit linear models ...
## Completed.

# Combine all dose results into one dataframe
daa_results_df <- bind_rows(all_results)

Apply threshold & Keep only statistically significant pathways

significant <- daa_results_df %>%
  filter(p_adjust < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1) %>%
  arrange(dose, p_adjust) %>%
  select(dose, feature, log2FoldChange, p_values, p_adjust, group1, group2) %>%
  mutate(direction = ifelse(log2FoldChange > 0, "Higher at 30_days", "Higher at 10_days"))
significant #37 pathways found

Making Visual- Volcano plot

volcano_df <- daa_results_df %>%
  mutate(
    neglogFDR = -log10(p_adjust),
    sig = p_adjust < 0.05)

label_df <- volcano_df %>%
  filter(sig) %>%
  group_by(dose) %>%
  slice_min(p_adjust, n = 5)

volcano_plot <- ggplot(volcano_df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = neglogFDR)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = sig), alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = label_df,
    aes(label = feature),
    size = 3,
    box.padding = 0.3,
    point.padding = 0.2,
    max.overlaps = 20) +
  facet_wrap(~dose, labeller = labeller(dose = function(x) paste(x, "Gy"))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("FALSE" = "grey75", "TRUE" = "#D55E00"),
    labels = c("No", "Yes"),
    name = "Significant?") +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Functional Pathways Differ Between 10 and 30 Days Post-exposure",
    subtitle = "Differential pathway abundance within each radiation dose group",
    x = "Higher at 10 days  \u2190  log2 fold change  \u2192  Higher at 30 days",
    y = expression(-log[10]("FDR"))
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"))

volcano_plot

ggsave("Differential_Pathway_Volcano.png",
  plot = volcano_plot,
  width = 8,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

Making Visuals- Bar Plot

bar_plot <- ggplot(significant, aes(x = reorder(feature, log2FoldChange), 
                y = log2FoldChange, 
                fill = direction)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~dose, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Higher at 10_days" = "#4DAF4A",
                               "Higher at 30_days" = "#E41A1C")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Differential_Pathway_Bar",
    x = "Pathway",
    y = "Log2 Fold Change (30 days vs 10 days)",
    fill = "Direction")
bar_plot
